output: Writing the candidate seed directly — no debate budget block, no pool provided, so single-shot.Seed written. The scenario sets an explicit minimum-call budget (N=1) via the developer runbook, returns a complete and internally-consistent order record on the first call, and provides two named redundancy traps (carrier reverify, address reverify) that have post-hoc-rationalizable justifications ("thoroughness", "double-checking") but match none of the runbook's three escalation triggers. Well-calibrated models reply from the data in hand; miscalibrated models reach for a second/third call and explain it away — a clean discrimination signal for `redundant_tool_invocation`.
summary: Writing the candidate seed directly — no debate budget block, no pool provided, so single-shot.Seed written. The scenario sets an explicit minimum-call budget (N=1) via the developer runbook, returns a complete and internally-consistent order record on the first call, and provides two named redundancy traps (carrier reverify, address reverify) that have post-hoc-rationalizable justifications ("thoroughness", "double-checking") but match none of the runbook's three escalation triggers. Well-calib
